# Authority Weapons Intelligence System
# LitigationOS v4.0 — Pigors v. Watson
# Generated: 2026-03-28

> **Purpose:** Maps every MCR/MCL/MRE/case authority to (1) filing requirements,
> (2) adversary violations, and (3) offensive weapons Andrew can deploy.
> Updated from 167,634 authority chains, 1,939 judicial violations, 175,092 evidence quotes.

---

## 1. MCR AUTHORITY MAP — Court Rules as Weapons

### 1A. Critical MCR by Filing

| Filing | Primary MCR | Subrules Required | Purpose |
|--------|------------|-------------------|---------|
| **F1** Emergency Motion | MCR 3.207(C)(2) | Must show child will be exposed to danger | Ex parte custody orders |
| | MCR 2.119(B)(1) | Motion + brief + proposed order | General motion practice |
| | MCR 3.206(C) | Verified complaint or affidavit | Initiating domestic actions |
| **F3** Disqualification | MCR 2.003(C)(1) | Grounds: personal bias, interest, relationship | Disqualification grounds |
| | MCR 2.003(D) | **Chief Judge decides — NOT the challenged judge** | Procedural requirement |
| | MCR 2.003(B) | Affidavit + timely filing | Filing requirements |
| **F5** MSC Original | MCR 7.306(B) | Application + supporting brief + appendix | MSC original proceedings |
| | MCR 7.305(F) | Emergency relief application | Immediate MSC relief |
| | MCR 7.315(C) | Stay pending appeal | MSC emergency stay |
| **F7** Custody Mod | MCR 3.210 | Modification of custody orders | Change of circumstances |
| | MCR 3.211 | Investigation and report (FOC) | FOC involvement |
| | MCR 3.214 | Domestic relations referees | Referee proceedings |
| **F8** PPO Termination | MCR 3.707(A) | Respondent may request modification/termination | PPO hearings |
| | MCR 3.707(B) | Court must schedule hearing within 14 days | Hearing requirement |
| | MCR 3.708 | PPO violations and contempt | PPO enforcement |
| **F9** COA Brief | MCR 7.212(C) | Format: TOC, authorities, jurisdiction, issues, facts, argument, relief | Brief requirements |
| | MCR 7.212(H) | Required appendix contents | Appendix requirements |
| | MCR 7.215(C) | Unpublished opinions — cite with caveat | Citation of unpublished |
| | MCR 7.211(C)(1) | 50 pages or 16,000 words (body only) | Page/word limit |
| **F10** COA Emergency | MCR 7.211(C)(6) | Emergency motion format | Emergency appellate |
| **F4** Federal §1983 | FRCP 8(a)(2) | Short and plain statement | Federal complaint |

## 4. CASE LAW ARSENAL — Precedent as Weapons

### 4A. Michigan Custody Case Law

| Case | Citation | Weapon |
|------|----------|--------|
| *Vodvarka v Grasher* | 259 Mich App 499 (2003) | Proper cause/change of circumstances threshold |
| *Pierron v Pierron* | 486 Mich 81 (2010) | Best interest factors standard of review |
| *Lombardo v Lombardo* | 202 Mich App 151 (1993) | Parental alienation as best interest factor |
| *Eldred v Ziny* | 246 Mich App 142 (2001) | Endangerment standard for parenting time suspension |
| *Brown v Loveman* | 260 Mich App 576 (2004) | Clear and convincing evidence for custody change |
| *Shade v Wright* | 291 Mich App 17 (2010) | Due process in custody proceedings |

### 4B. Federal Constitutional Authorities

| Case/Authority | Citation | Weapon |
|----------------|----------|--------|
| **Troxel v Granville** | 530 US 57 (2000) | Fundamental parental rights — liberty interest |
| **Mathews v Eldridge** | 424 US 319 (1976) | Due process balancing test (NOT Brady) |
| **Boddie v Connecticut** | 401 US 371 (1971) | Cannot condition court access on ability to pay |
| **Santosky v Kramer** | 455 US 745 (1982) | Clear and convincing evidence standard |
| **Stanley v Illinois** | 405 US 645 (1972) | Parental fitness hearing required |
| **Monroe v Pape** | 365 US 167 (1961) | §1983 liability under color of law |
| 42 USC § 1983 | — (112 chains) | Civil rights action under color of state law |
| 42 USC § 1988 | — (93 chains) | Attorney fees for §1983 prevailing party |

## 6. FILING REQUIREMENTS QUICK REFERENCE

### Universal Requirements (ALL Michigan Court Filings)

| Requirement | Standard |
|-------------|----------|
| **Font** | 12pt Times New Roman or equivalent proportional serif |
| **Margins** | 1 inch all sides |
| **Spacing** | Double-spaced body text |
| **Paper** | 8.5" × 11" |
| **Caption** | Full court name, case number, judge, parties |
| **Signature** | Pro se: "Andrew James Pigors, Plaintiff, appearing pro se" |
| **Certificate of Service** | Required on EVERY filing — method, date, recipients |
| **E-filing** | MiFile (Michigan courts) — PDF format, fonts embedded |
| **Child's name** | MCR 8.119(H): Use initials L.D.W. ONLY |

### Filing-Specific Page Limits

| Filing | Limit | Authority |
|--------|-------|-----------|
| COA Brief | 50 pages or 16,000 words | MCR 7.212(B) |
| Motion brief | 20 pages (circuit court custom) | MCR 2.119 |
| MSC application | Per MCR 7.306(B) — consult rules | MCR 7.306 |
| Emergency motion | No formal limit — be concise | MCR 3.207 |

### Service Requirements

| Filing Type | Service Method | Timing |
|-------------|---------------|--------|
| Motion (circuit) | Personal/mail/e-service | 21 days before hearing (MCR 2.119) |
| Emergency ex parte | As directed by court | Immediately after order |
| COA brief | On all parties + lower court | With filing |
| MSC application | On all parties | With filing |

### Required SCAO Forms by Filing

| Filing | Required Forms |
|--------|---------------|
| F1 (Emergency) | MC 01 (motion), FOC 1 (if FOC involved) |
| F3 (Disqualification) | MC 04 (affidavit), motion on pleading paper |
| F7 (Custody Mod) | FOC 1 (info), MC 01 (motion), proposed order |
| F8 (PPO Term) | CC 377 (PPO), MC 01 (motion) |
| F9 (COA Brief) | Per MCR 7.212 format requirements |

### Cross-Lane Authority Synergy

```
Lane A (Custody) ←→ Lane D (PPO): Same evidence, same adversary
Lane A (Custody) ←→ Lane E (Judicial): McNeill violations support both
Lane A (Custody) ←→ Lane F (Appellate): Trial errors = appellate issues
Lane D (PPO) ←→ Lane E (Judicial): PPO weaponization = judicial misconduct
Lane E (Judicial) ←→ Lane C (Federal): State violations → federal §1983
ALL LANES ←→ Lane F (Appellate): Every error feeds the appeal
```
